If groups of carnivorous birds should follow the army of a king
wishing to attack his enemy, his army would take to its heels; if the
birds fly in front, he would be victorious.
[The metre is Tsfl^TT. The desidcrative word is not quite
correct, as the root is aTT^reb The proper form is
?T5i srrca H*rc HW'
If at sunrise or sunset an army resembling an aerial city obst-
ructs the Sun's disc, one should predict that the king would be
involved in a dangerous war in the near future.
[Cf. TUT —
*R«n tn?i I

402 Bfhat SamhitU
It should be remembered that the army mentioned here is not a real
one, but an apparent one formed by a peculiar arrangement of the
clouds.
The metre is an unnamed variety of got by replacing the
penultimate long syllable by a short one in the metre.]
TO H MRV9M
A twilight is laudable if it is clear, has gentle breeze blowing
and is resounding with the pleasant cries of birds and beasts, not
